mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during stable employment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that immediately penalizes cyclical equity valuations, generating negative returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in 70% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or rolling Rank IC drops below zero for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers inflation fears, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and immediate negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in 60% of threshold-crossing months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (Kruskal-Wallis p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains flat, signals immediate consumer demand shocks that rapidly depress equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in 65% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production growth above 1.5% quarter-over-quarter during early recovery phases signals robust manufacturing demand, lifting equity valuations and producing positive returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in 60% of triggered instances during early recovery, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
